channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising Fuel Prices Impact Consumers Amid Declining Crude Oil Crisis
item.description: Fuel prices for gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel are rising despite a decline in crude oil prices, creating significant costs for travelers and threatening to undermine inflation control efforts. Analys...

item.title: BBC News Pays Tributes to Late TV Presenter Dermot Murnaghan
item.description: Dermot Murnaghan, a renowned British TV presenter and journalist, has passed away at the age of 68 following a battle with stage four prostate cancer. His family announced his death on social media, s...

item.title: England dominate Fiji with 11 tries to end losing streak in Nations Championship
item.description: England delivered a commanding 73-8 victory over Fiji in their Nations Championship clash, ending a five-match losing streak with a display of attacking dominance. The match, played at the Hill Dickin...

item.title: Vice Ganda's Performance Leaves tatay Roman Delighted on Everybody Sing
item.description: The article highlights Vice Ganda's recent performance on the television show 'Everybody Sing,' where his interactions with the show's host, tatay Roman, generated significant audience engagement. The...

item.title: Community Shocks Over Suspected Murder of Ann Widdecombe
item.description: The tiny village of Haytor Vale, known for its safety and tight-knit community, is reeling from the suspected foul play in the death of former government minister Ann Widdecombe. Found dead at her Dar...

item.title: Ben Mallah's Journey from Humble Beginnings to Real Estate Empire
item.description: Ben Mallah, a Kenyan-born real estate investor and entrepreneur, has built a massive empire in Florida through strategic investments and bold decisions. Born in 1965 in Queens, New York, he grew up in...

item.title: England and Norway draw in FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final
item.description: In a tightly contested FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final match at Miami Stadium, Norway and England played to a 1-1 draw after extra time. Norway's Andreas Schjelderup opened the scoring in the 35th m...

item.title: Paolo Maldini Named Technical Director of Italian National Teams
item.description: Paolo Maldini, the legendary Italian footballer, has been appointed as the technical director of the Italian national football federation (FIGC). This role involves overseeing the entire youth nationa...

item.title: The Water Tech of a 2002 Surfing Game Still Stands Out Today
item.description: Kelly Slater's Pro Surfer, a 2002 action-sports game developed by Treyarch, has gone viral on social media due to its impressive water simulation. Despite being over two decades old, the game's wave m...

item.title: FIFA Removes Miami Gardens Stadium From 2026 World Cup Amid Controversy
item.description: In a surprising decision, FIFA has removed the Miami Gardens stadium from its official list of venues for the 2026 World Cup. This move comes after intense scrutiny over the venue's capacity, infrastr...

item.title: India and New Zealand Strengthen Strategic Partnership with Trade Target of ₹35,000 Crore by 2030
item.description: India and New Zealand have formalized a strategic partnership aimed at boosting bilateral trade to ₹35,000 crore by 2030. The agreement, announced during high-level discussions, includes commitments t...

item.title: Trump Criticizes Democratic Leadership Over Shift Toward Left Wing Ideologies
item.description: President Donald Trump intensified his criticism of the Democratic Party on Saturday, accusing 'Radical Left Lunatics'—a term he frequently uses for Democrats—of being led by 'loud and unattractive pe...

item.title: BSP pushes PERA as supplementary retirement option for workers
item.description: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has emphasized that the Personal Equity and Retirement Account (PERA) should serve as a complementary retirement savings tool for workers, alongside existing Soci...

item.title: Czech teen Linda Noskova claims Wimbledon victory
item.description: Linda Noskova, a 21-year-old Czech tennis player, has made history by winning her first Grand Slam title at Wimbledon. In a closely contested final against fellow Czech Karolina Muchova, Noskova's vic...

item.title: Nita Ambani Recognized for Leadership in Shaping India's Economic Landscape
item.description: Nita Ambani, Chairperson of Reliance Foundation, has been ranked No. 1 on the Fortune India Most Powerful Women 2026 list for her visionary leadership and commitment to inclusive development. The list...

item.title: Ottawa experiences extreme rainfall and storm impacts on Canada Day
item.description: The City of Ottawa faced severe weather conditions on Canada Day, with a storm classified as a 1-in-200-year event. Over 167 mm of rain fell in certain areas, leading to significant disruptions. GO Tr...

item.title: Severe Flooding in Occidental Mindoro Due to Enhanced Monsoon Rains
item.description: Severe flooding has hit Occidental Mindoro after enhanced monsoon rains linked to the Habagat wind system, exacerbated by the remnants of typhoon Inday. The disaster has displaced thousands, damaged i...

item.title: Pan-Atlantic University emphasizes ethical communication in local governance
item.description: Pan-Atlantic University (PAU) has called on local government media officers to uphold the highest standards of ethics, accuracy, and transparency in public communication. This call was made during a c...

item.title: Nigerian Striker Toni Payne Signs With Inter Milan Ahead of 2026 WAFCON
item.description: Super Falcons midfielder Toni Payne has completed a move to Inter Milan, signing a two-year contract with the Italian club after leaving Everton. The 30-year-old, born in 1995 to Nigerian parents in A...

item.title: Springboks secure victory over Scotland in Nations Championship match
item.description: The Springboks delivered a thrilling yet error-strewn performance against Scotland in the second Nations Championship match of the season, securing a 42-28 victory at Loftus Versfeld. Despite Scotland...
